channel_replies_sample.text: Click this link https://boot.dev/?promo=FERN and use our code FERN to get 25% off your first payment for boot.dev (ad)
Taking care of your health just got easier, thanks to our sponsor Zocdoc. Start here: https://zocdoc.com/fern (ad)
We're working on something new. Join the newsletter now: https://roots.watchfern.com/
Hey Brilliant has just launched a new tutor and you can access it for free: https://brilliant.org/fern

channel_replies_note: IMPORTANT: no genuine conversational replies from @fern-tv to individual viewer comments were found on any of the 4 videos. On each video the only comment authored by the channel itself is a single pinned top-of-thread post -- either a sponsor-ad plug (boot.dev, Zocdoc, Brilliant) or a channel announcement (newsletter launch). All 'more replies' threads were expanded (17-20 per video) and cross-checked for the ytd-author-comment-badge-renderer; the only true hits were these 4 pinned comments. Note: many nested replies show a 'creator heart' indicator (heart icon on a fan's comment) which is a different signal (channel liked a comment) and does NOT mean the channel replied in text -- this was verified and excluded from the count. Fern's channel appears to NOT engage in text-reply conversations with commenters at all, unlike some smaller documentary channels. Tone stats below are therefore computed on the pinned-comment sample only and should be read as 'announcement/promo voice', not 'conversational reply voice'.

tone_analysis.we_i_ratio: Uses 'we' exclusively (We're working on..., our sponsor..., no first-person singular 'I' found) -- consistent corporate/team voice, never a named individual persona in on-camera comment text (though community posts are occasionally signed 'David, Jonas & the team').
tone_analysis.note: reply_rate and question_back_rate are 0 because zero actual replies-to-viewers were found in this sample of 4 videos (~78-89 top-level comments each, ~30 expanded replies each). This is a genuine content-gap/tone signal, not missing data.

recurring_viewer_asks.ask: Commentary/jokes about being 'bought out' or 'owned by a corporate/private equity firm' and surprise/approval that the channel still covers bold or controversial topics anyway
recurring_viewer_asks.frequency: seen on 2/4 videos (Why Putin Is So Hard to Kill, How Israel Kills Journalists)

recurring_viewer_asks.ask: Praise specifically for tackling taboo/controversial geopolitical topics (Israel-Gaza) and joking fear the video will be 'taken down' or the channel 'silenced' -- strong appetite for hard-hitting current-events coverage
recurring_viewer_asks.frequency: seen on 1/4 videos heavily (How Israel Kills Journalists), but thematically consistent with the corporate-ownership comments above

recurring_viewer_asks.ask: Nostalgia/requests to bring back the 'FERN' branded intro/audio sting from older videos
recurring_viewer_asks.frequency: seen on 2/4 videos (The Kid Who Hacked America, Why Putin Is So Hard to Kill)

recurring_viewer_asks.ask: Named-narrator recognition and preference -- viewers track and comment on which narrator (e.g. 'Jonas') is doing a given episode, implying narrator identity/rotation is a noticed and valued production detail
recurring_viewer_asks.frequency: seen on 1/4 videos (How to Escape Auschwitz), but a recurring theme across Fern's broader comment sections historically

recurring_viewer_asks.ask: National/cultural pride threads -- when a video covers a specific country's history or hero (e.g. Polish WWII resistance), a wave of same-nationality viewers thank the channel for representation/accuracy
recurring_viewer_asks.frequency: seen on 1/4 videos (How to Escape Auschwitz)
